channel.title: NPR Topics: Space
channel.description: NPR coverage of space exploration, space shuttle missions, news from NASA, private space exploration, satellite technology, and new discoveries in astronomy and astrophysics.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: NASA's Artemis II crew are quite the photographers. See what they've snapped so far
item.description: Many of the photos that have come out of the moon mission so far were taken by crew members. NASA says the crew is getting guidance from scientists on what to capture when they get closer to the moon.
item.content:encoded:
Many of the photos that have come out of the moon mission so far were taken by crew members. NASA says the crew is getting guidance from scientists on what to capture when they get closer to the moon.

(Image credit: Reid Wiseman)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: NASA's Artemis II mission is sending a toilet around the moon for the first time ever
item.description: For the first time, NASA is sending a toilet around the moon. The Artemis II astronauts' experience with this lunar lavatory will provide key data for future missions.
item.content:encoded: For the first time, NASA is sending a toilet around the moon. The Artemis II astronauts' experience with this lunar lavatory will provide key data for future missions.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Big tech's next move is to put data centers in space. Can it work?
item.description: In orbit, power is free. But everything else is expensive.
item.content:encoded: In orbit, power is free. But everything else is expensive.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: NASA's Artemis II has left Earth's orbit, and 4 astronauts now head to the moon
item.description: With the last major firing of its engine, the Artemis II spacecraft is now on a path that will take it around the moon and back.
item.content:encoded:
With the last major firing of its engine, the Artemis II spacecraft is now on a path that will take it around the moon and back.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: What's so cool about the far side of the moon? A NASA Artemis II scientist explains
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with NASA's Kelsey Evans Young, the Artemis science flight operations lead, about the rigors of space and the lunar slingshot to get home.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with NASA's Kelsey Evans Young, the Artemis science flight operations lead, about the rigors of space and the lunar slingshot to get home.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel J. Sánchez

item.title: Artemis II's journey to the moon is just getting started. Here's what to expect
item.description: Astronauts are now heading to the moon for the first time in more than 50 years. Here's what they have planned over the course of their nearly 10-day flight.
item.content:encoded: Astronauts are now heading to the moon for the first time in more than 50 years. Here's what they have planned over the course of their nearly 10-day flight.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Success! After key milestone, Artemis II astronauts speed off toward the moon
item.description: NASA's Artemis II crew has successfully launched on a mission that will take it around the moon and back to Earth. A key maneuver Thursday night sent them hurtling toward their lunar target.
item.content:encoded:
NASA's Artemis II crew has successfully launched on a mission that will take it around the moon and back to Earth. A key maneuver Thursday night sent them hurtling toward their lunar target.

(Image credit: Jim Watson/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: NASA astronauts have launched on a historic journey to the moon
item.description: NASA's long-awaited Artemis II mission launched Wednesday with four astronauts on board. Their capsule is orbiting Earth, and if everything checks out, NASA will send them off toward the moon.
item.content:encoded: NASA's long-awaited Artemis II mission launched Wednesday with four astronauts on board. Their capsule is orbiting Earth, and if everything checks out, NASA will send them off toward the moon.
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: NASA prepares to send four Artemis II astronauts on a lunar flyby
item.description: NASA is preparing to launch four people on a trip around the moon and back. It's the first time in more than a half-century humans are set to venture around the moon.
item.content:encoded: NASA is preparing to launch four people on a trip around the moon and back. It's the first time in more than a half-century humans are set to venture around the moon.
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: Liftoff! NASA astronauts have launched on a historic journey to the moon
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission lifted off from Kennedy Space Center in Florida at 6:35 p.m. Eastern Wednesday. The mission aims to send four astronauts around the moon on a roughly 10-day journey.
item.content:encoded:
NASA's Artemis II mission lifted off from Kennedy Space Center in Florida at 6:35 p.m. Eastern Wednesday. The mission aims to send four astronauts around the moon on a roughly 10-day journey.

(Image credit: Bill Ingalls)
item.dc:creator: Amina Khan
